chief_complaint: A 32-year-old woman presented with acute chest pain.
history: The patient had been taking combined oral contraceptives for four months. She presented with acute chest pain and ST-segment elevation. Background: Spontaneous coronary artery dissection (SCAD) is an increasingly recognized cause of acute myocardial infarction (AMI) in young women, with hormonal factors potentially playing a significant role.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings not provided.
labs: Coronary angiography revealed diffuse LAD narrowing that improved after intracoronary nitroglycerin. Follow-up optical coherence tomography at 6 weeks confirmed intramural hematoma consistent with SCAD.
differentials: Myocardial infarction caused by atherosclerotic plaque rupture
Myocardial infarction caused by coronary artery thrombosis
correct_ranking: Myocardial infarction caused by atherosclerotic plaque rupture
Myocardial infarction caused by coronary artery thrombosis
